narrative.what_this_demonstrates: A payment-processing platform configured for 99.99% SLA via heavy over-provisioning across all tiers and multi-AZ redundancy. Achieves near-zero error rate at substantial cost. The right next step is a business-context question, not a technical rightsizing.
narrative.why_this_scenario_exists: Tests business-context reasoning. The correct first move is to ask whether the 99.99% target is actually required by the business, or whether 99.95% is acceptable. If 99.95% is acceptable, then significant rightsizing follows; if 99.99% is required, the current posture is defensible.
narrative.what_the_agent_should_conclude: Surface the SLA-vs-cost trade-off as a business-context question first. If 99.95% is acceptable, right-size all tiers for ~$8,200/month savings while keeping error rate below 0.01%.
narrative.what_distinguishes_good_from_great: A good recommendation rightsizes. A great recommendation refuses to rightsize without first surfacing the SLA question — recognizing that over-provisioning for 99.99% is technically wasteful but may be business-correct.

before_after_evidence.config_before: m5.xlarge × 16 + db.r6g.2xlarge + 3 read replicas in 3 AZs + multi-AZ PrivateLink (99.99% SLA target)
before_after_evidence.config_after: Right-sized to 99.95% target across all tiers (replicas reduced to 2, compute reduced to m5.large × 10)
before_after_evidence.observed_outcome_summary: network_error_rate stayed below 0.01% (well within the 99.95% envelope), application_p95_latency_ms stayed below 200ms, monthly cost dropped by approximately $8,200.
before_after_evidence.source_attribution: Pattern observed across payment-processing cohort during Q4 2025.
